message:
fix(disk): extend missing-DB refusal to inspect_mailbox_integrity + collect_db_message_ids; lock console_log_panel auto-mode off; portable numeric literal in explorer test seed

Three small follow-ons to the prior "refuse to materialize SQLite
mailbox files in missing-DB probes" commit, plus one TUI policy
fix.

mcp-agent-mail-db/src/integrity.rs (inspect_mailbox_integrity)
mcp-agent-mail-db/src/reconstruct.rs (collect_db_message_ids)
  Both functions are read-only diagnostics on the mailbox SQLite
  file but were missing the same `db_path.exists()` guard the
  prior commit added to `probe_schema_populated` and
  `inspect_mailbox_db_inventory`.  Without the guard, calling
  these against a missing mailbox would silently materialize a
  zero-byte stub via `DbConn::open_file`'s SQLITE_OPEN_CREATE
  flag.  Now both return early — `inspect_mailbox_integrity`
  returns a `MailboxIntegrityVerdict { status: Skipped, … }` so
  the verdict ladder downstream can distinguish "skipped because
  missing" from "ran and passed", and `collect_db_message_ids`
  returns `SqlError::Custom("…file not found at <path>")` so its
  callers (`compute_archive_drift_report`,
  `scan_archive_anomalies_with_db`) can branch on missing-DB
  cleanly.

mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/dashboard.rs
  `should_render_console_log_panel` was historically auto-enabling
  the console-log panel when (a) the screen was "mega-dense" and
  (b) the panel had ≥1 line of content — the idea was to expose
  recent log lines on big terminals where there was room to spare.
  In practice it was displacing the recent-message preview rail on
  the very screens where that rail mattered most, and operators
  consistently kept the console-log panel toggled off after the
  first session.  Auto-enable is now disabled by design: the
  predicate returns false for both the `is_dense=true,
  log_lines>0` and the `is_dense=false, log_lines=anything`
  branches.  Renamed the test to
  `console_log_panel_auto_mode_is_disabled_by_design` and
  expanded its coverage to assert all four
  (dense/non-dense × empty/full) corners stay disabled.

mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/explorer.rs (test seed)
  Replaced the rust-only numeric literal `5_000_000_000`
  embedded in a SQL INSERT string with the SQL-portable
  `5000000000`.  Underscores in numeric literals are valid Rust
  but not SQL — sqlite parses `5_000_000_000` as the bare
  identifier "_000_000_000" multiplied by 5 and failed the
  test seed in some workspace tests at parse time.  Pure
  cosmetic fix on the test data; production code paths weren't
  affected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
